title: What it takes to solve the Hubble tension through Modifications of Cosmological Recombination II: in light of ACT DR6 and DESI DR2
abstract_en: We construct data-driven solutions to the Hubble tension, in light of recent data from the Atacama Cosmology Telescope (ACT DR6) and the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI DR2). We search for the minimal modification to the recombination history through a time-varying electron mass $m_e(z)$ that increases the best-fit $H_0$ inferred from CMB data toward the SH0ES value, without worsening the fit to the data. Using Planck and ACT data including lensing, we find a perturbative modification to $m_e(z)$ that fully resolves the Hubble tension, with the solution sharing the same qualitative oscillatory structure as in previous work using Planck data alone, demonstrating its robustness to the inclusion of more precise and independent CMB data. As a byproduct, the solution also eases the $S_8$ tension. Once DESI DR2 BAO data are added, however, perturbative modifications to $m_e(z)$ cannot fully resolve the Hubble tension. This reflects the same fundamental limitation: raising $H_0$ by modifying recombination generically lowers $Ω_m$, being inconsistent with late-time cosmological observations.
summary_cn: 本文利用近期ACT DR6和DESI DR2数据，探索通过随时间变化的电子质量（m_e(z)）来修改复合历史，以缓解哈勃张力。研究发现，使用Planck和ACT数据（含透镜效应）时，一种微扰性m_e(z)振荡结构可完全解决该张力，并同时缓解S_8矛盾；然而加入DESI DR2的BAO数据后，该方法无法完全解决，原因在于通过修改复合提高H_0会降低Ω_m，与晚期宇宙观测不一致。
one_sentence: 本文通过引入随时间变化的电子质量扰动来修改复合历史，利用最新CMB数据（Planck+ACT DR6）成功缓解了哈勃张力，但加入DESI DR2重子声学振荡数据后无法完全解决该张力。

title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
abstract_en: Parameter-efficient finetuning methods based on spectral decomposition have enabled progress in Continual Learning. In this paper we introduce TailLoR, which utilizes the singular bases U and V of the pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame to learn a low-rank update applied to the singular value matrix. A soft spectral penalty discourages updates aligned with dominant singular directions, reducing interference while routing fine-grained adaptation into the highly flexible, long-tail spectral coordinates.
summary_cn: 本文提出TailLoR，一种基于谱分解的参数高效持续学习方法。它利用预训练权重的奇异基U和V作为固定参考，仅对奇异值矩阵进行低秩更新，并引入软谱惩罚，弱化沿主导奇异方向的更新，从而减少任务间干扰，将精细调适引导至灵活的长尾谱坐标，实现高效的持续学习。
one_sentence: 本文提出TailLoR方法，通过将预训练权重的奇异基U和V作为固定参考框架，仅在奇异值矩阵上施加低秩更新，并引入软谱惩罚抑制主导奇异方向上的更新，从而在持续学习中实现低干扰、高灵活性的微调。

title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
abstract_en: For a humanoid robot to be deployed in the real world, the choice of command space (i.e., the interface between task planning and whole-body control) is crucial. Existing whole-body controllers typically demand dense kinematic or spatial references that planners struggle to synthesize from task semantics. We instead propose a compact, explicit interface that is intuitive, general, modular, and expressive enough for diverse manipulation skills. To this end, we introduce HANDOFF, a single humanoid whole-body controller that follows this interface and is distilled via multi-teacher KL distillation under a context-conditioned gating scheme into a mixture-of-experts student from three complementary specialists: whole-body motion tracking with safety-filtered data, locomotion, and fall-recovery. On the Unitree G1, HANDOFF matches state-of-the-art velocity tracking and offers one of the largest robust manipulation workspaces. We further demonstrate hardware feasibility through multiple natural-language-driven task roll-outs, powered by a VLM-driven agentic planner with no task-specific data or controller fine-tuning.
summary_cn: 针对仿人机器人全身控制中命令空间设计的关键问题，本文提出了HANDOFF，一种基于紧凑显式接口的全身控制器。该接口兼具直觉性、通用性和表达力。通过上下文条件门控机制下的多教师KL蒸馏，HANDOFF将运动跟踪、行走和跌倒恢复三个互补专家的知识整合为混合专家学生模型。在Unitree G1机器人上的实验表明，HANDOFF在速度跟踪和鲁棒操作工作空间方面达到最优水平，并基于VLM智能体规划器实现了自然语言驱动的硬件部署，无需任务特定数据或控制器微调。
one_sentence: 本文提出了一种名为HANDOFF的紧凑、直观且通用的全身控制接口，通过多教师知识蒸馏和混合专家模型，使仿人机器人无需任务特定数据或控制器微调即可执行多样化操作任务。

title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
abstract_en: Code language models need repository-level context to resolve imports, APIs, and project conventions. Existing methods inject this knowledge as long inputs (retrieved through RAG or dependency analysis) or through per-repository fine-tuning and LoRA -- costly at repository scale and brittle to evolving codebases. We introduce Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework that generates repository-specific LoRA adapters, effectively injecting repository knowledge with zero inference-time token overhead. Code2LoRA supports two usage scenarios: Code2LoRA-Static converts a single repository snapshot into an adapter, suitable for comprehension of stable codebases; while Code2LoRA-Evo maintains an adapter backed by a GRU hidden state updated per code diff, suitable for active development of evolving codebases. To evaluate Code2LoRA against parameter-efficient fine-tuning baselines, we build RepoPeftBench, a benchmark of 604 Python repositories with two tracks: a static track with 40K training and 12K test assertion-completion tasks, and an evolution track with 215K commit-derived training and 87K commit-derived test tasks. On the static track, Code2LoRA-Static achieves 63.8% cross-repo and 66.2% in-repo exact match, matching the per-repository LoRA upper bound; on the evolution track, Code2LoRA-Evo achieves 60.3% cross-repo exact match (+5.2 pp over a single shared LoRA). Code2LoRA's code can be found at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/code2lora-6857; the model checkpoints and RepoPeftBench datasets can be found at https://huggingface.co/code2lora.
summary_cn: Code2LoRA是一种超网络框架，用于为代码语言模型注入仓库级上下文。它通过生成仓库特定的LoRA适配器，避免了传统方法中长输入或逐个仓库微调的高成本。该框架支持静态快照（Code2LoRA-Static）和演化代码库（Code2LoRA-Evo，基于GRU隐藏状态更新）。在包含604个Python仓库的RepPeftBench基准上，静态跟踪达到了63.8%的跨仓库精确匹配，逼近逐个仓库LoRA的上限；演化跟踪则实现了60.3%的跨仓库精确匹配，比单一共享LoRA提升5.2个百分点。
one_sentence: 本文提出Code2LoRA，一个超网络框架，通过生成仓库特定的LoRA适配器来注入代码仓库知识，从而在推理时实现零额外令牌开销。

title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
abstract_en: Robot manipulation alternates between low-risk transit phases that call for fast execution and high-risk contact stages that demand slow, precise motion. Yet existing Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) only inherit a single fixed speed from training demonstrations. Prior efforts to accelerate VLAs through model compression, KV-cache reuse, or reinforcement learning only shift the policy from one fixed speed to another, and leave deceleration almost unexplored. We observe that the magnitude of each predicted action already governs how fast the robot moves, opening a direct route to controllable execution speed. We turn this observation into TempoVLA, a single VLA whose execution speed is controlled by an explicit condition. TempoVLA combines two coupled components. (1) A data-side Variable-Speed Trajectory Augmentation (VSTA) that re-times demonstration to any target speed by merging or splitting actions while preserving its motion semantics. (2) A model-side conditioning mechanism that feeds the speed to the policy. Statistics show that VSTA reaches the requested speed with negligible motion error. Experiments in simulation and on real-world tasks demonstrate that TempoVLA achieves flexible speed control in both directions, while VSTA additionally boosts the default $1\times$ performance via better data utilization. Furthermore, by cooperating with a large multimodal model, TempoVLA realizes dynamic speed control, accelerating through low-risk phases and decelerating for high-risk ones.
summary_cn: TempoVLA是一种创新的视觉-语言-动作模型，通过速度条件控制和变速度轨迹增强技术，实现了机器人执行速度的灵活调节。它能在低风险阶段快速运动，在高风险接触阶段慢速精确运动，同时通过数据利用提升默认速度性能，并可与大型多模态模型协作实现动态速度控制。
one_sentence: 本文提出TempoVLA，通过数据增强与条件机制实现对机器人执行速度的灵活双向控制，解决了现有视觉-语言-动作模型仅支持单一固定速度的问题。
